events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate scenario: pump and motor laser-aligned while suction/discharge piping disconnected. After piping bolted up, coupling gap changed visibly and laser report not repeated. Crew wants grout base and install coupling guard. Need sequence violation, likely pipe strain. Decision fail because non-compliant/installed-defective. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective, maybe non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60? Need include actions: repeat laser alignment after piping fully connected and bolted to spec, check pipe strain, verify soft foot, then grout and guard. References API 686. Need produce valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 55,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Final shaft alignment was performed before piping was connected, so the as-aligned condition is not representative of the installed operating condition.",
    "Piping bolt-up caused a visible change in coupling gap, indicating pipe strain and/or movement of the pump/motor from the aligned state.",
    "No repeat laser alignment was performed after piping connection, so there is no verified record that the coupling is within tolerance.",
    "Grouting the base and installing the coupling guard before confirming final alignment would lock in a misaligned condition and create a potential machinery reliability hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not grout the base or install the coupling guard until final alignment is verified.",
    "Inspect piping fit-up for flange alignment, bolt stress, and pipe supports to identify and relieve pipe strain.",
    "Recheck and correct soft foot on both pump and motor feet.",
    "Repeat laser shaft alignment with all suction and discharge piping fully connected, bolted, and supported as in operation.",
    "Document the post-piping laser alignment report and compare to vendor/API tolerances before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "API 686 requires shaft alignment to be performed with the machine in its final installed condition, including piping connected and supported, because pipe strain distorts the casing and shifts the shaft position. A visible coupling gap change after bolt-up proves the original alignment is no longer valid. Grouting or guarding now would accept a defective installation and risk bearing, seal, and coupling damage.",
  "references": ["API 686 - Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design"]
}
